events.headline: Atlantic International appoints Guus Franke as CEO as Jeffrey Jagid transitions to President; company to rebrand as Circle8 Group (CIRC)
Powerfleet board authorizes new $30 million stock repurchase program over 24 months
InnSuites Hospitality Trust continues reverse merger discussions and pursuit of strategic alternatives as part of NYSE American compliance plan following stockholders' deficit notice.
Interactive Strength announces 1-for-7 reverse stock split effective June 30, 2026 to regain Nasdaq $1.00 minimum bid compliance
Innventure CEO Gregory Haskell to retire effective Oct. 1, 2026; Board appoints Dr. William Grieco as new CEO and Class I director.
Magnachip appoints Chae Lee as CEO effective July 1, 2026; Interim CEO Camillo Martino steps down but remains Chairman.
Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory appoints former board member Al Harper as Interim CEO and Principal Executive Officer for a 180-day term, effective immediately.
Lands' End appoints Charlie Cole as CEO and board member effective July 13, 2026, replacing departing CEO Andrew McLean.
Forum Markets extends existing buyback program through June 2027 but reduces authorization from $250M to $100M and adds derivative repurchase authority.

Middleby confirms spin-off of its Food Processing business as Midera Food Processing, on track for completion July 6, 2026, with Midera securing a $1 billion credit facility ahead of becoming a stand-alone public company.
Honeywell Aerospace completes spin-off from Honeywell Technologies (HON), begins trading on Nasdaq under HONA on June 29, 2026, with a 1-for-2 distribution ratio to HON shareholders.
Middleby's spin-off of Midera Food Processing remains on track for July 6, 2026, as Midera secures a $1 billion credit agreement ahead of separation.
Corteva announces post-separation boards for Corteva and Vylor, Inc. as Vylor files Form 10 for spin-off of Corteva's seed business into an independent publicly traded company.
Honeywell completes spin-off of Honeywell Aerospace (HONA) on June 29, 2026, distributing 1 Aerospace share per 2 HON shares; remaining company renamed Honeywell Technologies.
Comcast announces tax-free spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky into an independent public company, expected to be completed in approximately one year.
GD Culture Group announces 1-for-250 reverse stock split effective June 29, 2026
Rockwell Medical implements a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of common stock, effective July 1, 2026.
BeyondSpring appoints Min Qiu as CEO and Na Li as CFO; amendment finalizes Qiu's compensation ($100K salary, 100K options).
Freedom Holding Corp. appoints Valeriy Kim as CFO, succeeding Evgeny Ler who moves to a special advisory role.
Fortune Brands appoints Jesse G. Singh as CEO; Interim CEO David V. Barry transitions to EVP and COO.
Beneficient removes interim designation, names James Silk as permanent CEO
Fortune Brands Innovations appoints Jesse Singh as Chief Executive Officer effective June 29, 2026.
Strategy Inc announces Digital Credit Capital Framework including a new class A common stock repurchase program and Digital Credit Securities repurchase program (size not disclosed in excerpt).
Theravance Biopharma to be acquired by Zymeworks for $17.00/share in cash plus a CVR on ampreloxetine proceeds, following a strategic alternatives review.
S&P Global spin-off of Mobility Global Inc. expected effective July 1, 2026, with Joseph Hinrichs appointed as incoming Board Chair.
GreenPro Capital approves 1-for-10 reverse stock split, expected effective on or about July 26, 2026.
HCW Biologics Board approves 1-for-6 reverse stock split, effective June 30, 2026
Quince Therapeutics announces 1-for-20 reverse stock split effective June 29, 2026, with split-adjusted trading to begin June 30, 2026.
Quince Therapeutics effects 1-for-20 reverse stock split, effective June 29, 2026, with split-adjusted trading to begin June 30.
FedEx Freight completed spin-off from FedEx Corp. and began trading as an independent company on June 1, 2026.
Old Second Bancorp board authorizes new $61.2M share repurchase program running July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027, with Fed nonobjection received.
ICF International Board approves $100M increase to share repurchase program, raising total authorization from $300M to $400M with ~$165M remaining available.
Darden Restaurants authorizes new $1.5 billion share repurchase program, replacing prior authorization.
Visteon board authorizes new $800 million share repurchase program expiring December 31, 2029.

Passage Bio announces definitive all-stock merger agreement with Remix Therapeutics, with concurrent $100M private placement; Passage shareholders to own ~7% of combined company plus a CVR for pediatric gene therapy milestones.
Autonomix Medical announces 1-for-21 reverse stock split effective June 24, 2026
Nuwellis announces 1-for-35 reverse stock split, effective June 25, 2026, with split-adjusted trading to begin June 26.
Daktronics Board authorizes additional share repurchases, increasing total available under existing program to $40.0 million.
First Merchants Board approves new $100M / 3.125M-share (~5% of outstanding) buyback program replacing prior March 2025 authorization.
JPMorgan Chase authorizes new $50 billion common share repurchase program effective July 1, 2026, alongside a planned quarterly dividend increase to $1.65/share.
Morgan Stanley reauthorizes a $20 billion multi-year share repurchase program with no set expiration, beginning Q3 2026, alongside a dividend increase to $1.15/share.
LiveOne expands stock repurchase program by over $7M, with approximately $5M remaining under the authorization
Massimo Group terminates previously announced FST acquisition, secures up to $4M in controlling shareholder funding to accelerate intelligent patrol systems development
Smart Powerr Corp. announces 1-for-10 reverse stock split effective June 16, 2026 to regain Nasdaq minimum bid price compliance
Imperial Oil renews NCIB to repurchase up to 24.18M shares (~5% of outstanding) over 12 months beginning June 29, 2026
Boundless Bio announces all-stock merger with Serapha Bio, with pre-merger Boundless stockholders to receive a $44–48M cash dividend and retain ~3.7% of combined company; closing expected Q4 2026.
Seadrill extends $500M share repurchase program through Dec. 31, 2026, with ~$208M remaining available
Middleby Board approves spin-off of Midera Food Processing; record date June 26, 2026, distribution July 6, 2026, 1:1 ratio, tax-free.
Middleby to spin off its Food Processing business as Midera Food Processing, Inc. (MFP), with a 1-for-1 tax-free distribution to Middleby shareholders of record as of June 26, 2026.
Boxlight Corporation announces 1-for-6 reverse stock split of Class A common stock, effective June 22, 2026, to regain Nasdaq $1.00 minimum bid compliance.
USBC majority holder approves reverse stock split at a ratio of 1-for-2 to 1-for-5, to be effected within 12 months.
Beyond Air board approves 1-for-20 reverse stock split following stockholder approval, effective upon filing of charter amendment.
Orchid Island Capital increases stock repurchase authorization by 25M shares to 26.6M total (~13.3% of outstanding); open-market/privately negotiated, no termination date.
Metropolitan Bank Holding board authorizes new $50.0M share repurchase program, replacing prior July 2025 authorization.

Jaguar Health's board initiates review of strategic alternatives, including potential mergers, acquisitions, and partnerships, aligned with anticipated NDA filing for crofelemer in rare intestinal failure diseases.

Jaguar Health Board initiates strategic alternatives review, including potential mergers, acquisitions, partnerships, and licensing deals, aligned with anticipated NDA filing for crofelemer in rare intestinal failure.
RYAM appoints new CEO as Board continues active strategic alternatives review with Morgan Stanley
VERSES AI discontinues AI operations and pursues strategic alternatives including potential merger or asset sale after failed fundraising efforts.
Alight announces 1-for-20 reverse stock split, effective June 30, 2026, with split-adjusted trading to begin July 1, 2026.
Middleby announces SEC effectiveness of Form 10 for Midera Food Processing spin-off, with record date June 26, 2026 and expected distribution on July 6, 2026 (1 share of Midera per share of MIDD).
Allurion Technologies files 1-for-15 reverse stock split effective June 17, 2026
Lakewood-Amedex Biotherapeutics announces 1-for-10 reverse stock split effective June 19, 2026
BitGo Holdings Board authorizes $50M share repurchase program (~8% of Class A shares), effective immediately with no expiration.

AstroNova to be acquired by Arcline Investment Management for $29.00 per share in an all-cash transaction with an enterprise value of approximately $272 million, following a strategic alternatives review.
Nano Dimension announces proposed business combination with Infinite Epigenetics, transitioning from 3D printing to AI-powered preventive health and diagnostics.

events.company: Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance, Inc.
events.headline: ARI board concludes strategic alternatives review and approves plan of complete liquidation and dissolution, subject to stockholder approval.
F&G announces CEO transition and formal process to explore strategic alternatives for subsidiary Peak Altitude
Honeywell Board approves spin-off of Honeywell Aerospace, with distribution expected June 29, 2026 (1 share HONA per 2 shares HON); remaining company renamed Honeywell Technologies.
Honeywell to spin off Aerospace Technologies business as Honeywell Aerospace Inc. (HONA), distributing 1 share per 2 Honeywell shares on June 29, 2026
All In FutureTech Alliance effects 1-for-6 reverse stock split of common stock, effective June 11, 2026.
Domo Board concludes a strategic transaction is the best path to maximize shareholder value and is in advanced negotiations regarding a potential sale.

Nano Dimension enters non-binding term sheet for proposed business combination with Infinite Epigenetics, an AI-powered preventive health and diagnostics company, following Board-led strategic alternatives review.
Comtech announces sale of most of its Satellite and Space Communications business to Gilat for $157.5 million as part of ongoing strategic alternatives process.

Adaptive Biotechnologies announces plan to separate its MRD and Immune Medicine businesses, evaluating strategic alternatives including a potential sale, partnership, or licensing transaction for Immune Medicine with a preferred path target
Tripadvisor agrees to sell TheFork to American Express for $700 million in cash, following its February 2026 strategic alternatives review.
Comtech agrees to sell most of its Satellite and Space Communications segment to Gilat for $157.5M, concluding its strategic review and pivoting to public safety as Allerium.
Braemar Hotels & Resorts concludes strategic review, will become a self-managed REIT and remain publicly traded after initially exploring a sale of the company.

events.company: Wheeler Real Estate Investment Trust, Inc.
events.headline: Wheeler Real Estate Investment Trust announces 1-for-4 reverse stock split of common stock, effective June 17, 2026
APPlife Digital Solutions announces 1-for-250 reverse stock split effective June 12, 2026

Eaton to separate and combine its Mobility Group with Dana Incorporated in a Reverse Morris Trust transaction valuing the combined company at over $10B, with Eaton shareholders to own at least 50.1% and Eaton to receive ~$1.1B cash.
Dana agrees to Reverse Morris Trust combination with Eaton's spun-off Vehicle and eMobility business (SpinCo); Eaton shareholders to own ≥50.1% and Dana shareholders ~49.9% of combined entity.
Verano completes 1-for-5 reverse stock split effective June 11, 2026, reducing issued shares to ~73.9M
Shuttle Pharmaceuticals effectuates 1-for-10 reverse stock split effective June 11, 2026
Nano Dimension to hold extraordinary shareholder meeting seeking advisory approval to continue its strategic alternatives review process, including any related Board-approved transaction.

Dot Ai announces LOIs for up to $5M convertible preferred stock investment and sale of a portion of its operating business for up to $6M cash plus assumption of up to $3M liabilities, resulting from its strategic alternatives review.
